revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Garandoya Kofun]][[ja:ガランドヤ古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Garandoya Kofun | native_name =ガランドヤ古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Garandoya Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Garandoya Kofun after reconstruction | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Garandoya Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.314028|N|130.905361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hita|qid=Q386968|1=Hita, Ōita}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|50.5|N|130|54|19.3|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Garandoya Kofun'''|ガランドヤ古墳|}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]]s, located in the Ishii neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Hita, Ōita|en|Hita, Ōita|qid=Q386968|lt= Hita|1=Hita, Ōita}} on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173475|title=ガランドヤ古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Garandoya Kofun cluster consists of three circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', located on the left bank terrace of the Mikuma River (upstream part of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}}) in the southeastern part of Hita City. Kofun No. 2 is located about 50 meters west of Kofun No. 1, and Kofun No. 3 is located about 25 meters southeast of Kofun No. 1. Two of these, No. 1 and No. 2, are both [[decorated kofun]], and are estimated to date from the mid to late 6th century. These tumuli is thought to belong to the decorated tumulus culture area of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}} basin, along with the nearby [[Anakannon Kofun]] and [[Hōonjiyama Kofun Cluster]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
; Kofun No.1 The mound of Kofun No.1 has been completely lost, leaving only the exposed stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. This is a rectangular stone chamber with a double-sleeved structure and a horizontal entry, approximately 4.3 meters long and 3.0 meters wide. On the back wall of the burial chamber, figures with outstretched hands, a horse, a boat, a bird, and a circular pattern are depicted in red and green pigments. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as horse harnesses, ornaments, iron arrowheads, earrings, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery were recovered, indicating that it was built in the latter half of the 6th century. An earth-covered concrete structure to protect the burial chamber was constructed in 2014.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
; Kofun No.2 Kofun No.2 retains its earthen mound, with a diameter of approximately 20 meters and height of 9 meters, but the eastern side of the stone burial chamber is exposed. The burial chamber is a horizontal multi-chamber cave-style stone structure, approximately 3.3 meters long. Although not in a good state of preservation, on the wall at the back is a mural depicting a horseman with a drawn bow, a concentric circle pattern, and a continuous chevron pattern, painted in green pigment on a red undercoat. Grave goods included straight iron swords, ornaments, and Sue pottery pottery. X-ray examination of sword revealed that it had an inlaid [[Japanese sword mountings#tsuba|sword guard]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Kofun No. 3 The site of Kofun No. 3 is currently used as a modern graveyard, and tumulus has been almost completely destroyed. It is not part of the National Historic Site, and it is unknown if it was once a decorated kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Garandoya Kofun are approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Hita Station|en|Hita Station|qid=Q5871577|lt=Hita Station|1=Hita Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|qid=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|1=Kyūdai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|qid=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hita.oita.jp/soshiki/kyoikucho/bunkazaihogoka/maizobunkazai/garandoyakofun/13462.html Hita City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
Garandoya Kofun<
{{Kofun navbar}}
